A law enforcement report that details the results of an investigation conducted by another law enforcement agency in which the Army is a party of interest, but for which the MP/DES did not have investigative responsibility or involves limited investigative activity by the MP/DES where a collaborative effort is not needed, a case number will be initiated.

A law enforcement report that allows administrative corrects to be made to a previously submitted Final LER. When administrative corrects are made to a Supplemental LER a subsequent Supplemental LER will be submitted.

Information disclosed to or obtained by an investigator that, considering the source and nature of the information and the totality of the circumstances, is sufficiently believable to indicate that criminal activity has occurred and would cause a reasonable investigator under similar circumstances to pursue further the facts of the case to determine whether a criminal
A law enforcement report submitted at the conclusion of all incident activity or completion of investigative activity.

A law enforcement report initiated upon receipt of credible information exists that a person or entity (to include unknown

A law enforcement report that is used to document all activity conducted through a specific date. The Interim LER is designed to provide Commanders, SJA or other official recipients with information that can be used as necessary to support administrative or judicial actions or for informational purposes, prior to the completion of the investigation.

A law enforcement report that documents the results of police or investigative activity conducted by military law enforce- ment jointly with another law enforcement agency(s). A Joint LER is initiated when Army interested in an incident exists simultaneously with the interest of one or more external investigative agencies, and a decision is reached by all agencies

An LER is the basic means of providing the results of police or investigative activity to supported commanders for infor- mation purposes and for administrative/disciplinary action, to first line supervisors for information and quality control, and
Army CRC for criminal information and long-term maintenance of investigative data.

A marking sometimes applied, in addition to or in conjunction with the marking FOUO, by the Department of Justice and other activities in the law enforcement community, including those within the DOD; denotes the information was compiled
A file containing fragmented or unsubstantiated criminal information or non-criminal law enforcement information.
SIR-final reports are submitted by CID at the conclusion of a CID criminal incident or investigative activity. 76 DA PAM 190–45 • 18 April 2019

An SIR-initial is the original submission of an SIR. Submission of an SIR will not be delayed due to incomplete infor- mation; all pertinent information known at the time of the SIR submission will be included. Additional required information
A selected criminal incident or investigation report that can be initiated and completed expeditiously.

An SIR-status is an “add-on” submitted to provide information not available at the time of the original report or when more pertinent information, such as results of autopsy, identification of subject, recovered arms, ammunition, and explosives or
An SIR-supplemental is a “correction,” submitted at any time to correct an error in a previous report.

A single point of contact within an organization or installation who oversees sexual assault awareness, prevention, and response training; coordinates medical treatment, including emergency care, for victims of sexual assault; and tracks the services provided to a victim of sexual assault from the initial report through final disposition and resolution.
